all_clusters.headline: Defying Cost Volatility: A Strategic Pricing Response in Zimbabwe’s Fragmented Economy. The Reality of Constant Price Instability in Zimbabwe
all_clusters.description:
Zimbabwe’s economy operates in a perpetual environment of cost volatility. Unlike more stable markets where inflationary shocks are episodic, Zimbabwe’s pricing landscape is shaped by continuous and overlapping disruptions, currency fluctuations, fuel price adjustments, import dependency, shifting monetary policy signals, supply chain instability, and periodic shortages of key commodities. For businesses, this means pricing is […]

all_clusters.headline: Chinese nationals flee Haroni River after exposé  – as community welcomes government intervention
all_clusters.description:
CHINESE nationals who devastated parts of Haroni River through illegal riverbed mining have fled and left their Rusitu Valley camp empty after being exposed in a collaborative investigation by NewZimbabwe.com and the Information for Development Trust (IDT). According to sources in Rusitu, only their wooden cabins and the perimeter-fence have been left, with previously seen […]

all_clusters.headline: Rights lobby group urges repatriation of citizens displaced in South Africa
all_clusters.description:
Channel Africa The Civic Engagement Forum of Zimbabwe has called on the Zimbabwean government to urgently intervene and assist in repatriating citizens displaced by recent anti-immigrant attacks in South Africa (SA). The organisation estimates that 146 Zimbabwean nationals have been displaced in Daggafontein, east of Johannesburg, following local raids targeting illegal mining operations, which subsequently […]

all_clusters.headline: Why South Korea is betting on Zimbabwe’s critical minerals and Libya’s oil recovery
all_clusters.description:
Business Insider Africa South Korea has asked Libya to support greater participation by Korean companies in its oil and refining sectors as Libya continues its recovery. Seoul is also seeking to strengthen economic ties with Chad and Zimbabwe through cooperation in education, infrastructure, healthcare and development assistance. Zimbabwe’s lithium reserves have emerged as a key […]

all_clusters.headline: Shopping in the Age of AI: Why Zimbabwe Risks Being Left Behind in the Data Economy
all_clusters.description:
For most of the twentieth century, shopping was a physical activity. Consumers left home, visited stores, compared products, sought information and completed purchases in person. Retailers competed primarily through location, assortment and pricing. By Brighton Musonza Artificial intelligence is changing this model fundamentally. Across advanced economies, consumers increasingly conduct product searches, compare prices, read reviews, […]

The post

all_clusters.headline: Gold Overtakes US Treasuries as World’s Leading Reserve Asset, Reinforcing Zimbabwe’s Gold-Backed Currency Strategy
all_clusters.description:
Gold has overtaken United States government bonds as the world’s most important reserve asset for central banks, underscoring a dramatic shift in the global financial system and lending fresh relevance to Zimbabwe’s controversial gold-backed currency reforms. According to a new report by the European Central Bank, bullion accounted for 27% of global central bank reserve […]

The post

all_clusters.headline: Zimbabwe trade deficit widens 16% as exports slump
Push to ring-fence sugar tax revenue for healthcare
Treasury nullifies Agriculture Ministry’s grain import levy – calls it legally invalid
all_clusters.description:
THE Ministry of Finance has declared as invalid a grain import levy imposed on millers by the Agriculture Ministry through Statutory Instrument (SI) 87 of 2025 and urged authorities to immediately repeal it. A letter dated March 3, from the Ministry of Finance’s permanent secretary George Guvamatanga to Chief Secretary to the President and Cabinet […]

The post

all_clusters.headline: China’s factory activity slows in May, raising questions over its economy
all_clusters.description:
BEIJING — China’s factory activity was flat in May, according to an official survey released Sunday, raising questions about how much further the country’s economy can shield itself from the fallout of the ongoing Iran war and pressure on demand. The official manufacturing purchasing managers index moderated to 50 from 50.3 in April, according to […]

The post Chi

all_clusters.headline: Parliament received over half a million public submissions on contentious Constitutional Amendment Bill
all_clusters.description:
Parliament received over half a million submissions on the contentious Constitutional Amendment Bill Number 3 during the window of public hearings, Chairperson of the Parliamentary Portfolio Committee on Justice, Eddison Zvobgo has revealed. The Minister of Justice, Legal and Parliamentary Affairs, Ziyambi Ziyambi, made the first reading of the contentious Constitutional Amendment Bill in Parliament […]
